steps.title: Use the contrast in legacy
steps.content: The first sentence frames Nixon mainly through Watergate. The underlined sentence begins with But and adds other parts of his legacy, including tribal sovereignty and health care reform, to show that the first perception is incomplete.

steps.title: Reject misread functions
steps.content: Choice A says the sentence explains why accomplishments are disregarded, but it gives counterbalancing information instead. Choice B says it compares two historical figures, but Obama is only part of a policy reference. Choice D points to Nixon's associates, who are not the focus of the underlined sentence. The answer is C.
